name: CyberSwaraj: Digital Privacy for Beginners
description:
[media: https://images.lumacdn.com/uploads/08/056156b2-1a35-49db-99ff-24677fc8bd72.png]
Forty years later, the feeling he was describing is just daily life. Your phone knows where you slept last night. Your browser knows what you almost bought and changed your mind about. Your keyboard knows what you typed and then deleted. None of this is a secret anymore, it's just something most of us have stopped noticing.
If you've ever had a faint sense that something about the internet doesn't feel right anymore, this session is for you.
​Privacy is not about hiding.
Most conversations about privacy start by making you feel paranoid. This one won't.
Privacy is something ordinary and human. It's the ability to decide what parts of your life get shared, when they get shared, and with whom. In the physical world this feels obvious. You close the door when you change clothes. You lower your voice in a crowded room. You don't hand your diary to a stranger.
Online, we've slowly given up all of that, usually without realising we were giving anything up. Each app asks for a small permission. Each service collects a small piece of information. Individually, none of it feels like much. Put together, it forms a surprisingly complete picture of how you live, think, move, and talk to people you love.
This session is a starting point for anyone who has never thought seriously about this before. No cybersecurity jargon. No lectures. Just a clear walkthrough of what's happening, why it matters, and what you can do about it this afternoon.
​What we'll cover
​
Part 1: Where privacy actually leaks
Why "I have nothing to hide" misses the point
The difference between privacy, secrecy, and safety
How ordinary apps and devices observe and track far more than you think
What changes, slowly and invisibly, when people lose their private space
​
Part 2: What you can actually do about it
One of our speakers runs an almost entirely open-source setup on his phone. No Google Play Store, no surveillance-heavy apps, just tools built by people who believe your data belongs to you. He'll walk you through what his setup looks like, how he made the choices he made, and the small realistic changes anyone in the room can make on their own phone before they leave.
After the showcase, we'll do a hands-on session together. You'll install a few open-source alternatives, replace a couple of the worst offenders on your device, and leave with a phone that's more private than the one you walked in with.
Privacy isn't all-or-nothing. Every small step counts.
​Who this is for
This is not a cybersecurity workshop. No technical jargon. Just honest discussion about something that affects every one of us.
This session is a starting point for anyone who hasn't deeply explored the need for privacy before.
If you've ever had a faint feeling that 
something about the internet doesn’t feel right anymore
, you'd fit right in the room.
​What is the 'CyberSwaraj' event series about
CyberSwaraj is a gathering for anyone interested in digital privacy, open-source tools, and taking back some control over their digital life. We run sessions for beginners, workshops for people ready to go deeper, and occasional deep-dives for the technically curious. Developers, privacy advocates, and people who've simply had enough of being tracked are all welcome.
[media: https://images.lumacdn.com/uploads/7z/b5a14bfe-4190-4eb1-8879-89c68d30b8b8.png]
Stay Connected
Join us on YouTube: 
https://www.youtube.com/@CyberSwaraj

After a short break, we’re excited to host our next meetup and return to our favorite format,  bringing people together through books, quiet reading, and good conversations.

name: From Bangalore to the Bay: The India→US Founder Journey
description:
The playbook for going global from India has changed. The infrastructure is better, the networks are denser, and US investors are paying attention in ways they weren't five years ago. But the hard questions haven't gone away: when to go, how to split the team, what you'll get wrong before you get it right.
This evening brings together founders who've made the leap, and an investor who's watched many of them do it for an honest conversation about what the journey actually looks like.
Agenda:
6:00 – 6:30: 
Registration
6:30 – 6:45:
 India → US investment trends (by Lightspeed)
6:45 – 7:30:
 Fireside Chat: Founders who have successfully navigated the India → US transition
7:30 – 7:45: 
Open Q&A
7:45 onwards: 
Networking
About Aspire:
Aspire
 is the all-in-one finance platform for modern businesses globally, helping over 50,000 companies save time and money with international payments, treasury, expense, payable, and receivable management solutions - accessible via a single, user-friendly account.  
Headquartered in Singapore, Aspire has 600+ employees across nine countries, clients in 30+ markets and is backed by global top tier VCs, including Sequoia, Lightspeed, Y-Combinator, Tencent and Paypal.
About Lightspeed:
Lightspeed
 is a global multi-stage venture capital firm focused on accelerating disruptive innovations in Enterprise, Consumer, Health, and Fintech sectors.
Over the past two decades, Lightspeed has backed hundreds of entrepreneurs and helped build more than 500 companies globally, including Anthropic, Mistral, Pika, Suno, Emergent, Sarvam, Zepto, Nutanix, Razorpay, and OYO.

name: Beyond Scaling: Making Large Language Models Efficient
description:
Transformer architectures have enabled the rapid progress of modern language models, but scaling them efficiently remains a major challenge. As models grow larger, compute cost, memory usage, inference latency, and deployment complexity increase significantly.
In this session, Abhay will discuss practical research directions to improve transformer efficiency in small and medium-sized language models.
The talk will cover architectural experiments, training optimisations, efficient attention mechanisms, memory-efficient techniques, and inference-focused design choices being explored while building open-source LLMs at FrontiersMind.
Speaker:
 
Abhay Kumar is a co-founder of FrontiersMind, an AI research lab focused on efficient small and medium-sized language models optimised for enterprise and real-world deployment use cases.
Hugging Face: - 
https://huggingface.co/FrontiersMind
LinkedIn:  
https://www.linkedin.com/in/akanyaani/
To attend online:
⁠Add to calendar: 
https://shorturl.at/rGZU1
Gmeet link: 
meet.google.com/uzb-mmdh-wjs
Pre-read:
⁠Basics of Transformer architectures
The Illustrated Transformer
Memory-Efficient Attention: MHA vs. MQA vs. GQA vs. MLA
Understanding DeepSeek's Multi-Head Latent Attention
Looking forward to seeing you!

name: Agent Dev-Sprint: Build, Deploy, and Battle
description:
Ready to move past basic prompting and build AI that actually 
does
 things?
Welcome to 
Agent Dev-Sprint
—a high-velocity, hands-on one-day event designed for developers, engineers, and AI builders to master autonomous agent architectures and put their code to the test in a live, gamified arena.
Whether you are looking to orchestrate LLM inference, implement advanced tool-use, or figure out how to keep agents from looping endlessly, this sprint bridges the gap between raw theory and production-ready execution.
🏎️ The Event Flow
We move fast. The day is structured into high-impact "Laps" to take you from a blank IDE to a competing agent by sunset.
Lap 1: The Warm-Up (Technical Deep Dives):
 Learn the core pillars of agent architecture—Perception, Reasoning (ReAct/Chain-of-Thought), and Tool-Calling.
Lap 2: The Straightaway (Hacking Phase 1):
 Set up your environment using our starter kits, build your core logic, and test local execution loops.
The Pit Stop (Lunch & Mentorship):
 Refuel, network, and unblock your code with hands-on help from our expert mentors.
Lap 3: The Final Corner (Optimization):
 Fine-tune your prompts, integrate custom tools, and run sandbox simulations to prepare for the tournament.
The Checkered Flag (The Arena Showdown):
 Deploy your agent endpoint to the live leaderboard and watch them battle it out head-to-head in a gamified environment.
🛠️ Guided by Experts. Built by You.
You won't be building in isolation. Throughout the day, you’ll have direct access to industry experts specializing in artificial intelligence and solutions architecture to help you debug your reasoning chains, optimize context windows, and sharpen your agent’s decision-making matrix.
🎮 Inside the Agent Arena
The grand finale of the Dev-Sprint is the 
Agent Arena
. Your custom-built autonomous creations will be unleashed into a live, gamified simulation environment. They will have to parse dynamic data, utilize APIs to make strategic decisions, and react to real-time events to climb the leaderboard and claim ultimate bragging rights.
📌 Logistics
📅 Date:
 June 20th, 2026
📍 Location:
 Amadeus, Bangalore
💻 Bring:
 Your laptop, charger, and a hunger to build. (Starter kits and supported framework boilerplate will be provided).
Spaces are limited to ensure high-quality mentorship and a stable arena environment. Secure your slot now, build your agent, and dominate the leaderboard!

name: Model to Machine — The Builder Session for Physical AI Infrastructure
description:
Model to Machine
A Builder Session on Physical AI Infrastructure
A closed-door gathering of founders, engineers, operators, and researchers building the infrastructure layer powering Physical AI in India.
From robots on factory floors and vision systems on assembly lines to autonomous vehicles in warehouses, the next wave of AI is moving beyond software and into the physical world. The challenge is no longer proving that AI works. It is building systems that can operate reliably in complex, unpredictable, real-world environments.
The questions facing builders today are fundamental:
Is embodiment a prerequisite for general intelligence, or merely one interface among many? Could a sufficiently capable disembodied intelligence generalize across arbitrary physical form factors, making embodiment largely irrelevant?
Is the future of robotics a general-purpose humanoid that adapts to the human world, or a collection of highly specialized machines optimized for specific tasks?
Is the robotics generalization problem fundamentally a data problem, or are there deeper limitations in current architectures and learning paradigms?
Session Format:
Unlike traditional conferences, this is a working session designed for practitioners.
🏭 Live Product Demonstrations
3-4 startups building at the frontier of Physical AI will demo working products and prototypes, offering a behind-the-scenes look at how these systems are designed, deployed, and operated.
🔧 Technical Deep Dives
Open discussions on the realities of building Physical AI: edge deployment, reliability, infrastructure, robotics stacks, computer vision, model orchestration, and operating in demanding industrial environments.
🤝 Perspectives from Operators
Founders and leaders from scaled Physical AI companies will share lessons from the field, hard-won insights, and the challenges they believe will shape the category going forward.
Agenda:
4:45 PM
 — Registration & Networking
5:00 PM
 — Opening Remarks — 3one4 Capital & AWS
5:30 PM
 — Live Demonstrations from Physical AI Startups
7:00 PM
 — Open Discussion & Networking
Hosted By
AWS, the cloud and AI infrastructure powering some of the world's most demanding industrial workloads, and 3one4 Capital, an early-stage venture capital firm backing category-defining companies across deep technology, industrial systems, and Physical AI.

name: Pipes, Streams, and Queries: Engineering Fast Data at Scale
description:
Join us in Bangalore for an amazing meetup at the intersection of streaming data and high-performance analytics. Whether you are building real-time pipelines, optimizing query performance at scale, or figuring out how to make streaming and storage work together in production, this event is for you.
Engineers and architects from ClickHouse and GlassFlow will share practical lessons from the field, covering everything from stream processing and ingestion patterns to sub-second query performance on live data. Connect with the Bangalore Streams community and walk away with ideas you can actually use.
Don't miss out! RSVP and secure your spot!
🗓️ Agenda:
09:30 AM: Registration & networking
10:30 AM: Welcome & opening
10:40 AM: Making Postgres Migration Boring With ClickPipes by Amogh Bharadwaj, Senior Software Engineer @ ClickHouse
11:10 PM: Talk 2 - TBD
11:30 PM: Break
11:45 PM: Talk 3 - TBD
12:15 PM: Closing Remarks
12:30 PM: Lunch & networking
If anyone from the community is interested in sharing a talk at future events, complete this 
CFP form
 and we’ll be in touch.
🎤 Session: Making Postgres Migration Boring With ClickPipes
Description
: Postgres-to-Postgres migration is still surprisingly manual and error-prone. Moving between providers like RDS and Cloud SQL often requires users to generate schema dumps themselves, and coordinate multiple fragile migration steps manually.
On top of that, initial loads can be slow, and many existing solutions lack good progress tracking, observability, and alerting — making migrations difficult to monitor and trust in production.
In this talk, I’ll introduce the PG-to-PG ClickPipes we’re building at ClickHouse to make Postgres migration far more seamless.
Speaker:
 Amogh Bharadwaj, Senior Software Engineer @ ClickHouse
I’m Amogh Bharadwaj, a Bangalore-based software engineer and BITS Hyderabad graduate. I was part of the founding team at PeerDB, a Postgres-first ETL platform acquired by ClickHouse, where I now work on managed Postgres and lead efforts around data movement via ClickPipes.

name: Building Enterprise AI Agents with RAG using UiPath SDK and LlamaIndex [Bengaluru Meetup]
description:
Agentic AI is reshaping how automation gets built — and this meetup is your practical introduction to building it with UiPath.
We'll walk through the UiPath SDK and CLI, explore LlamaIndex as a retrieval framework, and unpack the fundamentals of RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) — the technique powering knowledge-aware AI agents. You'll also see how UiPath Skills can package reusable capabilities that plug directly into your agents.
Come ready to learn, ask questions, and connect with others navigating the intersection of RPA, LLMs, and agentic automation.
Day's Discussions:
Introduction to coded agents
What makes an agent "coded" vs visual- when to use each approach and where agentic AI is heading
UiPath SDK — quick walkthrough
Core SDK concepts, activity packages, and how to invoke automation from code
LlamaIndex overview
Data connectors, query engines, and why LlamaIndex is the go-to framework for LLM-backed data retrieval
RAG fundamentals
Chunking, embedding, vector stores, and retrieval — the building blocks behind any knowledge-augmented agent
UiPath CLI for coding agents
Scaffolding, publishing, and running agents from the command line — the dev workflow end-to-end
UiPath Skills for agents
Packaging reusable capabilities as skills and exposing them to your agent at runtime
Observe the speakers build a UiPath coded agent & go back home to try it yourself
Step-by-step: wire up SDK + LlamaIndex + RAG into a fully working coded agent
📅 Date:
 Saturday, 11th  July
⏰ Time:
 10:00 AM – 12:30 PM
📍
Location:
 7th Floor, UiPath, 
Vajram ONYX, Devarabisanahalli, Bellandur, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560103
Can't wait to meet you :P

name: PLG Breakdown: Uber
description:
Join us for an exclusive, product-manager-only session where a  Product-Led Growth (PLG) professional will uncover untapped growth levers within Uber.
This deep-dive session will focus on using PLG frameworks to enhance user activation, engagement, and retention—ultimately driving sustainable revenue growth.
Through case studies, data-backed insights, and actionable strategies, attendees will learn how to identify opportunities across the product funnel and design experiments that directly impact business outcomes.
Highlights:
Discover practical PLG opportunities tailored to Uber's user journeys
Learn how to align product decisions with revenue outcomes
Get frameworks to drive growth without heavy marketing spends
Open Q&A and peer discussion with fellow product managers
Who Can Attend:
This event is exclusively open to 
Product Managers
 passionate about driving growth through product innovation.
---
Note: We are not associated with Uber, their product is being used to demonstrate opportunities for educational purpose only.
